config.title: SARK Technical Screening
config.description: A short technical quiz for evaluating web fundamentals, product thinking, and engineering judgment.
rules: Answer every question before final submission.
You can move back and update responses before submitting.
Do not refresh the page during an active attempt.

resultInvite.title: Join the SARK quiz circle
resultInvite.description: Scan the WhatsApp QR to join the group for updates, discussions, and next steps.

questions.prompt: Which browser task is most directly handled by JavaScript on the main thread?
questions.description: Pick the answer that reflects how interactive UI logic usually runs before worker offloading.

questions.prompt: What makes an API response easiest for a frontend team to evolve safely?
questions.description: Think in terms of predictable shape, explicit semantics, and lower regression risk.

options.label: A stable schema with clear nullable fields
Dynamic keys that mirror user-entered strings
One large HTML payload for every view

questions.prompt: Why do engineering teams separate frequently edited content from UI layout code?
questions.description: Think about maintainability, review speed, and reducing accidental UI regressions.

options.label: It separates editable content from layout code
It removes the need for route files

questions.prompt: Which future backend step matters most before accepting public quiz submissions?
questions.description: Assume the admin flow arrives later and answer integrity becomes meaningful.

questions.prompt: What visual choice best matches the SARK direction for technical credibility?
questions.description: The UI should feel sharp and deliberate, not noisy or theatrically animated.

options.label: Strong hierarchy, restrained accents, dense information
Five unrelated neon gradients at once
Large empty marketing blocks before the actual tool
